stemHtml: An aircraft has just flown for 5 hours at FL410, and is performing a fast turnaround to depart again shortly. The fuel gauge shows -11ºC and the OAT is +2ºC. A warm front is approaching the airfield and the aircraft will depart through drizzle. What will the pilot see on the walk-around and what risks of icing should be discussed during the briefing?

options.a: Hoar frost will be present initially, but will quickly disappear due to the positive OAT.
options.b: There will be no icing on the ground due to the positive OAT, and no icing risks are present on the departure.
options.c: Clear ice will form on the ground and cannot be removed by de-icing fluid.
options.d: Fuel frost will be present on the ground, and the drizzle may freeze to the wing on departure.
